title: Relative Clauses
description: Join two ideas into one with relative pronouns: who for people, which for things, that for either, where for places and whose for possession — defining clauses that take no commas, and the object pronoun you can leave out.
sources: CEFR B1 descriptors (Council of Europe, Common European Framework of Reference)
Raymond Murphy, English Grammar in Use (relative clauses: who/which/that, where/whose, leaving out the relative pronoun)
